title: Had to Send Back because of Static Issues and Microphone Complaints.
text:
I had to send back because I had a weird static issue in the one earbud and people complained about the microphone quality. Based off of other reviews, I think I just got a bad set of the Ankers. Bottom line: I was disappointed in these, compared to my other two sets of earbuds. There are better "true wireless" earbuds out there, even for the price.

Below is a review based on the three sets earbuds that I have (or have tried for extended periods). The three sets include: these Aukey earbuds, Anker's Soundcore Life P2, and Sony's WF-1000XM3. Of the three, Aukey is by far the easiest to carry around, due to the case size, and is far cheaper than the rest. I use the my Aukey's for my work-phone calls, and while doing messy projects, while my Sony's for relaxing and listening to high quality music, and for personal phone calls. I used to use the Ankers for work phone calls, until I returned them. I tried not to let the probable defects interfere with my judgement... to a point.<

title: Expected better
text: It's sounds like one of the buds is muffled. Whatever the issue, the sounds is not clear. It's disappointing because they are really comfortable and it's hard to find ear buds that are comfortable for women. I tried walking with them on a treadmill at a slow pace and it sounds like something in rattling in the right bud. I would expect that however the one that sounds muffled is the left. Guess I will need to continue my search...

title: Better than most if you're sitting still, keeping them in your ear while walking not so much
text: These work well for the majority of my needs, which is sitting in my office on video calls, and the sound quality is very good and people can hear me well in most cases. Like just about any Bluetooth device, when the battery starts to get below 50%, there is an echo, but this is not unique to these earbuds. The good news is that the battery lasts through multiple calls, back-to-back if necessary, and they seem to charge very quickly in the charger/storage container. The noise canceling is OK, but not great, for instance, if outside with a little wind, but not bad for indoors. The biggest problem I have with them is that I can't get them to stay in my ear if I'm moving around much at all. I've tried just about every different ear bud that come with them, which are several, and they always work out of my ear when I'm moving around. A second problem is that the button to hang up is right where I naturally put my fingers on the ear bud to reposition them securely in my ear and if I'm not e

title: I bought my wife the Expensive Brand, and bought myself the Budget Brand we were surprised
text:
So I waited a while to post this review, I've owned these earbuds for about 4 months now. These work just as good as new (I have taken care of them, I haven't dropped them, they haven't hit water, etc.) I've treated them like I treat my expensive sunglasses, etc. and they amazingly work just as good as the day I bought them. Which leads me to believe a lot of the high-value Items I buy that last longer, is this because they are constructed better or because I subconsciously treat them better and with more care because they cost so much?
Anyways back to the Review.

Audio for Calls: 10/10 - easy to hear, clear, no lacking anything in this department

Audio for Movies: 8/10 - This is not like putting on a pair of Bose Headphones, but this is almost as good as putting in a pair of Bose Ear Buds. (Not for Bass, this is their biggest downfall) but for the dialogue, highs/mid tones the audio is 10/10 for base it's 5/10 and for an avg we get the 8/10 (This is based on

title: THEY FALL OUT - not reccomended
text:
Do not buy. who ever engineered the design did a horrible job.

1. The earbuds fall out, constantly! They fall out while eating, walking, bending over, even sitting down and just studying. I tried all 4 different sizes for rubber plug, but none worked to properly secure the ear bud in my ear.

2. The earbud charge station horrible! the charge station case fell ~2 feet from my desk to carpet and the case opened and my earbuds randomly rolled out in different directions. Imagine dropping the case in public and losing ur $50 earbuds down the drain.

3. The button on the earbuds are places on the back side. The problem with the button being big and on the back side is it keeps getting pushed puasing my podcast every time I have to readjust the earbuds in my ear.

Overall, I reccomend not buying these $50 earbuds for the poor design in both the case and the earbuds. I needed to conceal my earbuds for work and was hoping these would be a decent, but no

title: Great or maybe not so much - what do you want earbuds for?
text:
Soundcore is a line of products from Anker. I was unable to find this Life P2 model on the soundcore.com site but didn't need any support so that was okay. The site/product line are apparently still under development as many of the products that appear say "coming soon!"

This is a set of two buds in a carry case that serves as a charge cradle as well as a small battery bank that can recharge the buds on the go. You connect the case to power with a USB type A male cable on one end and a USB type C cable on the other (supplied).

Operating instructions are small-print hieroglyphics that you may need a magnifying glass to decipher. Nothing unusual there. Fortunately these things don't require a lot of setup so a quick once-over will probably be the last time you need them.

First off, take the buds out of the case and peel off the little paper connector covers to expose the charge points. Then put the buds back in the case, connect power and wait until

title: Terrific.  Top-notch so far.  And a great price point.
text:
Fantastic. I was not a believer in these kinds of earphones. This primarily came form an experience with a set from about two years ago that I found inadequate in several regards. Wow.

1. What's in the box?: You receive the earphones inside a case, plus some extra ear tips (small, large and extra large), and the usual instructions.

2. Set-up? You are told to charge the box for about 5 minutes. Once I did that, I pulled these out and hit the button and was able to connect with my phone.

3. Use?: Once set-up, I immediately started listening to music. These things re quite good. The fit is great, I used a different set of ear tips and frankly it is like I can't feel then at all. As to the sound, it is very good and has what is termed "passive noise cancelling" which really means that the thing is not going to block the world but considering you will use this outside in situations where some listening is required, this is fine. You cannot tur

title: Too much of a crapshoot, quality-wise.
text:
I've returned these twice for two different issues: Battery and general troubles.

The first set worked perfectly, but one battery would only reach 70% or so. It was clearly charging (from 10% to 70%) but never fully.

The second set hit 100%, but had all sorts of connection issues — multiple copies (yes, I removed the old pair), sometimes one side would connect, sometimes both. See the image for one example.

When it finally DID connect to the PC, it had an awful buzzing sound at anything more than about 55% volume. (Other Bluetooth earbuds did not have this issue.) It was fine connected to my phone.

After two tries, as good as the sound quality was, I'm moving on.

title: A Good Bang For Your Money
text: I tried other earbuds for several days along with these. I was very picky including comparing the price and value. In my opinion, these gave me the best fit (not too big or not too small), pretty good quality in sound and even better for the price. The reason why I gave it a 4 star is a personal preference. I like my sounds/music really loud. I specifically use my earbuds for the gym (like loud music) and while riding my Harley (need to be loud due to the elements of noise). In my opinion,, it could be a little louder. But then again, they are not headphones. They do stay in place, even when sweating or riding on the expressway. The controls are easy to use and not easy to accidentally press. The best part is that the microphone(s) work well when you're talking on the phone unlike other brands that sound like you're far away. I hate when you're talking to someone and it sounds like they are muffled or super far away. These are very clear when using the phone. Also, the battery lasts fo

title: Does not place nice with apple. See all the sponsored reviews!
text: Cannot pair with iPhone 6 8 or X. You have to forget the Bluetooth device and hope the phone sees it. If you have paired to different phones in the past you have to turn Bluetooth off on other devices in range. Sometimes only one earbud would show up to pair. Not worth the frustration.

title: Heavy sweater? Not for you
text: Sound distorts. I use these while I am drumming and working out and after a month, max volume has already gone down in quality. Do not recommend

title: Clear Crisp sound
text: My niece has been wanting a pair of airpods for a while now. Unfortunately, they come with a hefty price tag. I seen these Soundcore Life P2 True earbuds, and seen they were compatible to iphones, I got them for her. Her words were "I don't know about airpods, but these work great." "The sound is crisp and clear." Where have these been all my life?

title: Excellent value for phone calls but lacks booming bass
text:
I've had issues with every pair of ear buds I've ever tried that I could afford except for these. The sound is clear and crisp, perhaps lacking a bit in bass but frankly I need these for phone calls not intended for listening to music.
Anker makes good, if not great, products and gives you tremendous value for the money. The only reason for the 4 in sound is lack of pulsing base, the battery life is absolutely amazing. Another solid Anker product.

title: Good sound but not great for using while on the move
text:
I bought these headphones as a cheaper alternative to a more robust pair of wireless headphones, like the pixel buds or airpods. Really, I bought them in late 2019 as a hold out for the pixel buds 2 and the USB C cable on my pixel 3 keeps giving out and I just didn't want to deal with the hassle of wired headphones any longer.

The sound quality is good. As someone who is used to listening on a respectable set of AKG 702s or Sony MDR-7056s they obviously don't stack up, but it's better than I would have thought to achieve for the price point, especially in the width of the soundstage.

My main gripe is the in-ear feel. They encompass your whole ear canal with sweaty, grippy plastic. The bad kind. Like the kind that makes it really hard to talk while wearing them, or when you move you hear a muffled rumpling with every step. I personally can't stand that and would prefer a more open ear feel, but that's just me. Just be aware of what you are buying, I find it hard to

title: Get the liberty Air 2s
text: I know the price on these look good, but they are not worth it, i have a Pair of Liberty Air 2s and they blow these out of the water, of course the price difference is there but, These have legitimately no bass AT ALL. Im upset that this is the lower version of what i have and they sound this bad, avoid these, even with a discount avoid them, save a few more bucks and get liberty Air 2s. Youll thank me later

title: Fall out of ear
text: Bought for wife for Christmas based on reviews. Have to say, they sound good. However, 2 stars because the basic function of ear bud is so that you can hear music. However, to actually hear music, what is job #1?? They must stay in your ear. Right? Well maybe that a personal matter, but these kept falling out of my wife's ear on walks and during workout. Her apples one stayed in. She tried all of the different size covers that came with the products, so we are returning and looking for another options.

title: Great for business use
text: I now own two pairs of the Soundcore buds. I own the Note and now the Life P2. I use them only for work. I have one pair at home, for when I work from home, and one pair in my work office. I use my iPhone 11 for work. These Anker Soundcore products work terrific. They pair very fast, last all day, and sound great while on the phone. I simply have no complaints about using them for work. Heck, even the microphones on each bud sound crystal clear, to the folks on the other end, talking with me. If you are using these for work, jump on them, as they are crazy good and work each and every time. I find they are also very comfy. They come with multiple size ear buds, so it is easy to make them comfy for your specific ear. Another benefit is each bud can be used independently of the other. I have never had a bud run out of battery, but it is super cool knowing I can just throw on the second bud and keep working, should I need to. I can't imagine ever needing to do that though, as these buds l

title: Stopped working before a year was up
text: They started off pretty good, but I started getting Bluetooth connectivity issues even when I was in the same room as my phone and I couldn't deal with it. Stopped using them for a while. I tried another brand and sent those back, so thought I'd pull these back out again to use as a backup. The headphones wouldn't connect to my phone at all! The left earbud was overheating... like uncomfortably hot! It wouldn't turn on or reset at all so I took it out of the case because I didn't want anything to overheat. The right earbud would connect for a few seconds, say it was at 100% battery, then power off. Then it stopped connecting all together. Usually I really love Anker's quality... But if you want earbuds that are going to last longer than a year, I suggest looking elsewhere.

title: Great for Conference Calls, Fine for Music
text:
I use these primarily for my excessive meeting schedule via videoconference (Microsoft Teams). I end up wearing these for 6+ hours a day with no discomfort. Audio quality is great both ways. My wife has these, too, and I hear her loud and clear. If phone and conference calls are your primary use, these are excellent.

For music, they are fine. They block out some noise, but are certainly not active noise canceling. Note the "noise canceling" in the produce description refers to blocking out ambient noise from the microphones during phone calls. Not blocking noise entering your ears like Sony or Bose. They are light on bass. For the price, I would still rate them good for music. If music is your primary use, these are fine but not excellent. I still use them around the yard and in the shop.

title: Hard to connect and poor battery
text:
Pros:

They’re comfortable.
The sound is okay.

Cons:

They don’t stay synched or one side synchs, but not the other.

After a week on the charger, they’re still only charged to medium.

title: Good, but 15% of the stated battery life
text: Decent, but not a 40h playtime life. The sound is good, much better than cheaper options. I get 5-6 hours of playtime from them when they have been fully charged.

title: easy to us
text: I really like these because I forget that I have them on. they are so comfortable. easy to pair and you don't have to use both at the same time. a great charging case that easy to carry in the pocket. I would recommend this product to others.

title: Pass on them
text:
Pros:
The sound is decent. I really like that it comes with multiple sized (whatever they’re called) so that they can fit your ear hole properly. And I also like that it tells you that battery level when you put them in your ear.

Cons:
The case is pretty large and bulky compared to other earbuds.
After a while of wearing, my ears hurt really bad. To get the best sound, I have to push them further into my ear to create that seal for noise cancellation , which makes the plastic part of the earbud push against my ear and it hurts after a while. I think the actual earbud itself is a little too large. Also, you have to place the earbuds back into the case in a particular way for the connectors to line up right. If they’re not, your phone will stay connected to them and it becomes really frustrating. If you disconnect, and then walk near where you have them stored, it’ll reconnect. While I do like them and plan on keeping them, I would not recommend them to anyone.

title: Get what you pay for.
text: Good pair of noise cancelling earbuds. Worth what you pay for them. The fit is a little weird in the ear.

title: Unimpressed
text: I was looking for a cheaper option for wireless headphones and came across these. They overall looked great and connected with no issues, however, they had zero bass when I listened to music and the mic quality was absolute garbage, so I can't use them at work. Returned after a week

title: Sound is Awesome
text: I was surprised and pleased these Bluetooth earbuds performed as well as they did. I use them on my laptop and devices. The recipients of the phone calls have told me the clarity of my calls were crisp and clear. I like that the ear buds give me several size options to choose from to fit my ear size. Go for it. My sister loved how I sounded when she called me and wanted to purchase a pair so I sent her the link. You can't beat the price and I love how they charge up in the small carrying case. I haven't had to charge the case yet. Yay guys and gals! You made the perfect pair for me.

title: Great earbuds for calls, podcasts, or audiobooks. Not the best for music.
text:
These things are great for work calls. The battery life is amazing, each earbud can easily go almost all day of non-stop calls. Plus the fact that you can use each one individually is a huge bonus.

The sound quality is great for things like calls, podcasts, and audiobooks. But I feel like they lack a little in the music department.

Also, for me, these are not great for working out. They work well for walking or jogging, but have trouble staying in while running or jumping rope.

Overall, great product for specific purposes.

title: Perfect for a cubicle
text:
****update 01.29.20****
After more than a week, I feel the silicone ear tips gets accustomed to your ear. And it is good set of headset for price. Bumping upto 5 stars.
The LEDs flashes are way too noticible while using it at night
To sum it up
pros
Amazing battery life.
Good sound with decent MIC.

Cons:
they pop out sometimes after doing some house chores.
Unlike the airpods they have buttons which can be slight pain when u have to answer calls or skip a track .
LED flashes like an airplane at night :)
**********

This sounds close to the Bose wireless headphones except at the bass level ( if you want to hear blaring heavy metal, the Bose beats it).
Sounds really good and mic clarity is pretty decent. But it keeps falling off if you are into any activity.
For test purposes, Kept my wife's airpod in one ear and the soundcore in the other. After couple minutes of house work the soundcore bud falls out.

title: Perfect for smaller ears, adjustable for different sizes
text: I didn't know I had small ears until I tried to make air pods happen and they always pop out of my ears. These have a better price tag, plus it comes with multiple sizes of silicone eat thingies. I was surprised to find I needed the smallest one, and these don't fall out!!! The sound quality is great, super easy to charge and hold a charge for a long time. The noise cancellation is actually too good..? I wouldn't run in them because I tend to run solo before the sun comes up and need to be able to hear my surroundings. Otherwise these are great.

title: Great sound
text: Ease of use is great, The ear pods fit perfectly and the sound is wonderful. You hardly know that their in your ears.

title: Fantastic
text:
Couldn't be happier. Ankr is a good name for a reason.
Sound quality is fantaatic, ear buds come with multiple size attachments, very comfortable!

title: Fantastic Sound, but falls off ear.
text:
The sound is amazing, they are very light, but easily fall off ear and because they are so light, will not notice if they do fall off.
Great battery life. Wish they would make a small clip to hold in place. Would have kept them.

title: Updated: Not worth the money
text:
Updated 1/18/20- Anker sent me a liberty new replacement for free and without asking. It sounds much much better as is the phone calls. Much more comfortable and conducive to changing the controls. Sadly no volume controls on the ear units. Battery life states about 5 hours and that is what I am getting. If I use it a lower volume it does increase though. I would give these a 3.5/5 stars because they are $50 without volume controls. If these were cheaper and the battery life was longer, it would be 5 stars.

Original: Most of the time, people could barely hear me on a phone call. Now if I adjusted the ear piece to where it was uncomfortable and hard to use to the controls, they could hear me fine. The ear piece covers never truly fir my ear. Sound quality was hit and miss. Sometimes the music sounded great and sometimes it was so-so. I think it based on how the ear piece was oriented. I returned these, as they weren’t worth how much j paid for them.

title: Great for work, music just OK
text:
Great battery life, great conversation clarity both hearing and speaking to others.

Quirky setup to get both buds to pair together.

Not the best sound quality for music. Some harshness in the highs and a lack of low-frequency response.

title: Super Sounding Buds! APTX powered HD sound.
text:
I insist on using Aptx Enabled Buds. This is a software/hardware system the enables the best of pure HD/CD AUDIO.
The Anker buds are totally Aptx and deliver KICKING audio performance! The buds themselves are comfortable and fit well. The touch controls are straight forward and allow you to manage the various functions. Best Buds I've used!

title: Best Little Ear Buds for the price..
text: These beautiful little Ear Buds are wonderful. I got a recommendation on utube. They work as good as extremely expensive ones. I do not regret my purchase. Quality is outstanding and they charge right in their case. Along with the case and charger cord there are multiple options sent to find your proper ear size so that they fit properly. I have small ears and most pairs fall out but these stay in place. They’re very easy to use even for a Senior like myself.  You can download the manual online, which I liked because I lose everything.

title: No bass
text: I am a huge music lover. I listen to all kinds of it and for hours a day. The first song I listened to with these headphones was Pillowtalk by Zayn, which is a bass-heavy song. I immediately noticed there was no bass to speak of with these earbuds. That's just not acceptable for me, so I'm returning them.

title: Great product - great customer service experience
text: I used these headphones for work on a daily basis for 5 straight months. I did not have any issues with hearing or being heard on calls. After about 5 months the right one would no longer charge so I contacted the seller through Amazon. I received quick follow up with some tips for troubleshooting. After trying the troubleshooting without success the seller sent me a new set which are working great. Awesome product, exceptional customer service. Definitely will be a return buyer.

title: Nice
text: Excellent for the price.  Not for audiophiles. Battery life is top notch.

title: Awesome
text: If you have purchased a Samsung product before then the name anker will sound familiar to you. These far surpassed the ones that came with my s10 plus. The earphones fit comfortably and unlike others the shape allows you to comfortable lay down while using. The sound quality is awesome and very loud while in ears but make no sound out. These have built in noise reduction that actually works and speakers of course for calling. The charging box is top of the line and uses USB c as most do now. Overall these are my new go to headphone.

title: Terrible pairing function..
text: Average sound quality, the pairing function is terrible, if you have more that one device pairing with the headphones ...You have many problems to pair each time that you want to change beetwin them...

title: Good bass and Long life battery
text: Deep bass and Battery life is quite good. One of the the pairs, the left side) got no sound after owning less than a month, but a very quick replacement from Amazon is quite awesome! Got them a a couple days. Thank you.  I still like to recommend it.

title: Pleased with this purchase
text: The sound quality on this product was great and well as the comfort when in hear. The only downside for me was the pairing is not as easy as I hoped (may just be me getting used to the device)

title: Quality for the price
text:
The headphones themselves are nice. They pair easily, and can easily use one or the other, or both. They pair automatically with the last device upon pulling them out of the case. You can easily go from pairing both, to one, by putting the other back into the case. The earbuds themselves are made well and the recharge case is nice and recharges plenty of times. I typically go about two weeks between case recharges. They sound good, but not studio quality or anything. If you're in an office and want some music in the background they work excellent.

I find that as I move frequently, they often feel like they are going to fall out and I have to continuously reposition them in my ears.

The biggest issue though is that I find these to be incredibly uncomfortable in my ears after an hour or more. I've tried all the different ear pieces on both, and they just get annoying after a period of time. I'm not sure if this is a personal issue due to my ears, or by the nature of

title: Most AMAZING Customer Service
text: These came highly recommended through an online newsletter, yet I was sadly disappointed (initially). The earbuds, even after switching out to different sizes, didn't stay in very well. After expressing this concern in a review, Anker's customer service reached out to me inquiring how they could fix the problem. They offered to send me their upgraded headphones at no cost, and informed me (with much pride) that along with engineers they seek out customer voices and opinions to help shape their products. I was absolutely floored with how personable they were, and how adamant they were about ensuring customer satisfaction. This is SO RARE to find these days, but ANKER did a phenomenal job and has earned a lifelong customer for it. HIGHLY RECOMMEND!

title: Great for price
text: Wanted a back up pair, these do the trick. Happy with overall product. Good price.

title: Does the job
text: Got these for my girlfriend for video calls and work. She loves the long battery life and the design of them make them unnoticeable with her hair down. I’m a little more of an audiophile and I wouldn’t really use them for my music. The construction is good and charged quick

title: Awesome.
text: Works just as good as the Apple AirPods but for way less than half the cost!!! Plus has a 40 hour battery life!! Perfect for runners

title: Easy to connect
text: Nothing bad to say. Great price for quality wireless earbuds.

title: I love them
text:
The sound is great . The Line of sight range is awesome .
They don’t fall out like some brands do while I’m working in the yard .

title: Good quality earbuds
text: Arrived on time, boxed up well. Ear buds as described.  The instructions are  a little vague but after a couple of attempts I got them paired to my smart phone ( I am not tech savvy). I really love them and use them when walking or exercising at the gym. As far as phone conversations go my son says he can hear me but my grandmother says she can hear me but my voice sounds different and I  can't use them when I am talking to her.

title: Sound doesn’t sync between headphones.
text:
Unfortunately can only use one at a time as the sound echos (delays?) between sides. + lots of interference when using both.
2ndly: When making calls, the ‘R’ side pod is the only bud am able to be heard on.
3rdly: Range isn’t very far. Am only able to step 3ft away from device when using. And if you go around a corner or door forget it, connection drops.
So, these are fine for listening to podcasts, using one at a time, if your device is close. But if you want to use both at once, or for calls, or need to be more than 3ft away from your device. These prob aren’t it.

title: Won't pair to my Ipod touch
text: Overall, I really likexperience these.  The only issues I've experienced are that I can't pair them to my iPod Touch and I sometimes have trouble keeping them from falling out of my ear.  Very good sound,  Good battery life and quick charging make this a great buy.

title: Very good sound
text: Easy to use, easy to pair, great sound, it comes 5 pairs of Ear Tips to choose the one that fits you better. Very good value for the price you pay.

title: Great noise canceling on phone calls
text: I have tried lots of wireless headphones. These are the best. They make the phone calls actually possible. Other headphones, the background noise is simply too much. Once the price is considered, these are a must buy. Customer service from Rochelle was quick and helpful too!

title: Works great!
text: My daughter pairs these with her iPhone 12, MacBook Pro, or  iPad just fine.  They always connect and they sound good.

title: Great unless you’re moving around
text: Sound and battery life are great. The problem is I cannot get any of the silicone ear plug sizes to fit comfortably and stay in my ear. It’s been very frustrating. Because of that not great for working out or running.

title: Great product and value
text: Excellent sound, ear comfort, charges quickly.

title: Anker Soundcore Life P2 vs. Anker Soundcore Liberty 2
text: I had first ordered the Life P2 earbuds and wasn't satisfied only because they didn't have a deep bass sound that I like while listening to music. I gave them to my daughter and she is using them while attending college. A lot of her classes are using zoom calls and she says the earbuds are awesome. I've received the new Liberty 2 in the mail and these are exactly what I was looking for. I was concerned about the fit since I bike a lot and don't want these falling out but these have a great fit in my ear (better than the previous ones). The sound is awesome with great bass and these are by far the best headset/earbuds I've ever owned.

title: Great Value!
text: Sure beats the Apple ones for twice or more the money I bought for wife and daughter, great product, even dropped one immersed in sink water once and keeps working.

title: Awesome! So glad I got these vs. paying up the wazoo for a more expensive brand!
text: These are so dope, and a fraction of the price of some of the more "trendy" brands out there. They stay in my ears when working out (running/walking/jogging, weights, stretching, yoga, etc.) and, sometimes, I even forget that I'm wearing them. As far as noise cancellation goes, I have to take 1, or both, out of my ear(s) in order to uphold a conversation with someone. Otherwise, the other person's voice is too muffled and I often miss or don't understand what he/she is saying to me. So i'd say that's pretty good as far as blocking out noise goes. I have yet to run out of battery life with either the ear buds or charging case, and i've had them for about 3 weeks already. Sound comes in crystal clear when answering phone calls, and sounds good to the person on the other line, as well. I usually end up putting people on speaker phone a lot and I was told that I come across much better through the ear buds. Lastly, I don't have to get tangled up in cords anymore! 🙌 As a bike commuter and w

title: so far so good
text: Fit is excellent - sound great - battery life much better then iPods

title: Great purchase
text: These sound good, easy to carry in your purse and charging is very easy.

title: Great Buds
text:
Love these things

I had a set of Liberty Lights, until i dropped the case right next to a sewer drain. I liked them, but battery life was poor, and sound quality was awful

These buds are terrific on battery. Sound quality is decent, and they form a good seal in my ear.

The only gripe is they're a pain to get out of the case. You have to be slow and methodical getting them out or you risk dropping them. I really haven't found a good way to get a grip on a bud when removing them from the case. Putting them back is no problem

I have a Pixel 2xl and i can use either bud right out of the case. I don't have to do any re-pairing. They're versatile for me in that regard. I really like them

title: Too many little irritating bugs.
text: I looked at so many of these types of wireless appliances and couldn’t find even expensive one with great reviews. I decided to try these to see if I even liked this style. Well I don’t. They are impossible to keep in the ear. I’ve tried the different sizes and it hasn’t helped. The left side is completely useless. It fell out three times in the first hour. So now it permanently resides on the charging case. The right one pairs immediately to my iPhone. It will not pair to my iPad nor my laptop no matter what I do. As I said it doesn’t stay in the ear so while I’m talking I have to adjust it to keep it from falling out. I’ve hung up on multiple people just adjusting it. Next is that once I put it in the charger it does not unpair. It’s a pain when I go to look at a video and there’s no sound. I have to manually shut it down. As a comparison my last pair stayed in my ear and let go of my connection when plugged in. Plus I could pair to any or all devices. Trouble is I can’t get that one

title: Definitely get your money's worth.
text: They sink right up to the phone, the battery life is a couple hours. The charger case charges them up nicely and fits in the pocket. The base isn't all that strong but I'm not getting a very tight seal with the cups provided. Upgrading to better tips would most likely improve the base but for the price I can't complain.

title: Best wireless ear buds
text: Anker soundcore life p2 are great wireless ear buds for a great price  clear crisp sound  I highly recommend

title: Pretty good
text: I have small ears and finding earbuds that will stay in are really hard to find. These stay in, sound decent, and mic works pretty well. They don’t seem to automatically pair with my iPhone and are lacking in touch controls by comparison to others I’ve tried, but considering they are about the only ones that don’t fall out every two minutes or need constant adjusting, I’ll consider it a win.

title: Does not stay in my ear
text: I can't get these things to stay in my ears even with the little buds. Going into thr drawer for no use.

title: Great value for solid wireless earbuds
text: These have been great so far after a few weeks. Pair easily with iPhone. Great battery life. Good noise reduction with great sound quality. I like them as much as my wife’s AirPods

title: So far so good...
text: After a few tries they seem to be of good quality. Let's see how long they last.

title: Good for the price
text: haven't used them a lot yet but so far they seem to be ok.

title: Comfortable, easy to use, decent sound quality.
text: These are very comfortable and come with 4 options for rubber inserts. The black is super sleek, I think they look much better than competitor's white ones (which look like q-tips). There are buttons on each ear for controlling the music and answering calls. It was easy to connect to both my computer and my phone and they don't fall out when I move around. I wish I could control volume from the earbuds rather than from my device. I also wish they would get a little louder. The sound quality isn't as good as some of the name brand competitors, but it is not bad.

title: Doesn't work well
text: Now for the second time it does not connect  as pairs only one  at a time . The left now does not stay connected  when the right  is charging.  Very disappointed.  Very disappointed.  Not good

title: Bad
text: I bought this as a gift and the receiver didn't like it at all. Terrible sound quality, it's going back.

title: Sound goes in and out
text: When I'm using the device sometimes the volume just goes very low making it difficult to hear

title: Poor sound long battery easy to pair
text: I thought I had hearing loss. Honestly, poor quality sound and dropped connectivity on and off all the time.  Easy to pair, good battery life, poor sound.

title: Good starter ear buds
text: Great product at a great price. Sounds great. Multiple tips for ears for different size ears

title: Good quality for price and performing as expected
text:
Positives:
+Sound quality is good for the price
+Battery life is strong so far (I’m expecting deterioration in performance in time though, so depending on when that sets in, I’ll update my review)
+Connects quickly and seamlessly to devices

Negatives:
-Noise cancellation is minimal (though, this wasn’t a feature I was expecting)
-Can’t get the perfect fit to my ears despite the fact that there are 4 different size attachments

title: some initial connection issues to start up but other than that great buy
text: Syncing could be more seamless.  incoming calls the ring volume is basically non existent havent figure how to change that yet.  Had for over 2 months now.  Solid purchase.  Definitely higher quality buds out there but for this price point, they have been been Ideal for running, jumping, or just to take calls.

title: 5⭐ for music & video chat
text:
Setup was surprisingly simple on my Samsung Galaxy and iPad. The connection doesn't drop.

These are exactly what I needed. Sure there's no volume control on them, but my phone's a pocket away to lower the volume anyway.

I ♥️ that it comes with 4 different sized ear tips too.

The lengthy battery life that comes with the case is great if you end up in a spot where charging isn't an option.

title: I really want to like these...
text: I have no other piece of tech that frustrates me as much as these. Specifically, trying to pair them. Then when they are paired, the sound breaks up all the time unless the phone is laying on the desk next to me. Every time I go walking, it breaks up.  I missed my return period, so I am stuck.

title: Good for Walking
text: Just got them but very clear sound, easily connected but unable to evaluate battery life at this time since I just got them. Noise can elation not the best but I will use them while walking so need to hear basic outside noise. Also have 5 different size ear pads.

title: Poor quality
text: The earphones stopped working in the first 45days.  It’s a poor built product

title: Good
text: Music sounds very good. My wife says the phone part sounds ok.

title: I don't need anything else
text:
These are so good. For those saying that they are tinny sounding without any bass then you didn't lock them in your ear as the image on this website shows. If you insert them, push them in and move them upward once in your ear then they pretty much lock into place.
One downfall is that you cannot pair these to multiple devices unless you are using it as 1 bud per device. For the price it's worth it. If I want these on a second device then I'll just buy another pair.
Anker has quickly become an industry leading brand. I own some of their other headphones and they are very good. I also own several of their powerbanks. Their customer service has always been top notch. I couldn't be happier with my first pair of wireless ear buds.
Lastly, the charging case where these sit in act as a charger even when it is not plugged in. You only plug in the charger to charge the base. Once you have done that then you can carry the base with the buds in it and they'll charge. This is way t

title: Nothing impressive.
text: I really wanted to like these, but ended up returning. It was an "okay" product for me. The design is sleek and battery life is great. But I have too many connectivity issues compared to the JLAB. Also, the phone calls aren't as crystal clear as advertised because the 4 microphones pick up unnecessary noise. Nothing else can be happening (typing, eating) or the caller complains of too much background noise.

title: Could not pair left bud. Update - great customer service solved issue
text:
Could not pair/sync left ear bud to laptop or phone. Right side worked fine. Returned for refund.
OK. Update! Anker actually read this review and personally emailed me apologizing the buds didn’t work and sent another pair free of charge! Sure!, why not?!
Got them within a week. Now these buds paired up easily. They come with 5 ear plugs to perfectly fit whatever ear size. These things have BASS! The treble is clear and bass is deep without distortion. Actually too much bass. The midrange sounded like it was in the back but a little adjustment of the EQ on the phone and it came up front. Sometimes it takes a little manipulation to get them seated in your ear just right to get that deep bass in good tune. Oh, and the sound cancellation really works. My wife was yelling at me and I couldn’t hear a thing outside of my music. Time will tell if these buds are reliable but so far, I’m impressed. You will also. Thank you ,Anker for the great customer service. (They re

title: Terrible for phone calls. Good for music.
text:
Sound quality for music is great and so is battery life.

These are useless for calls. I sound quiet and muffled to people I call and even with their call volume maxed it's still quiet. Background noise is also still able to come through so the noise canceling doesn't quite work that good.

title: It probably doesn't get much better at this price point
text: I've tried a TON of bluetooth headphones at various price points. I don't think it's worth spending a ton of money. I live in a large city, and if I'm walking through the most heavily populated area I lose bluetooth connection no matter what brand of buds I buy. That being said, these Anker buds are worth the price. They have a great sound, the battery lasts seemingly forever (they pumped out at least 2.5 hours while I was waiting in line at the DMV) and they're comfortable. The great part is that the case uses a USB-C charger, which a lot of bluetooth buds do not. Don't expect Bose quality sound and you'll be happy.

title: They perform as described
text: I bought them because they stayed in the ear!

title: Fantastic earbuds!
text: Great sound! Lost lasting charge! Price is great too! I would recommend Anker products to everyone.

title: Keeps falling out of my ear.
text:
These do not fit securely in my ear no matter which bud size I use. I’ve ready other reviews with the same complaint. The slightest move of my head will dislodge them from my ear. I wish they came with wing tips.

Too bad because they sound great. The sound is very well balanced.

title: Sound fair support lacks but ok for price
text: Sound is ok, not great listening to music, but for the phone it works great. Listening to music I would say sound is fair if your into music, everything else It is a good buy.

title: Awesome product
text: Great ear buds great battery life awesome Bluetooth range

title: Buy it!
text: amazing value! For the money you can't make a better decision.. sure there are headphones that have better features or more features but this is a great bang for the buck

title: My everyday audiobook earbuds
text: The best sounding wireless earbuds I had for under $50. Works independently, easy to pair, easy to play/pause. Long battery life, now I use this for my audiobook every day, 6 hours a day without ear pain, unlike those expensive one I had.

title: Great buds for great price
text: I bought these for myself after getting much more expensive ones for my daughters. These work at least as well as theirs. I am using them every day. The battery life is fantastic. The only thing I don’t like is what I guess is supposed to be noise cancellation. I can’t hear anything around me, but I also can hardly hear anything besides my chewing or whatever is going on in my head. Consequently I never use both at once. I use one at a time.

title: Excellent
text: I must just say that these are top notch and comparable to any of the more upper name brands that cost a lot more money. And for the price that they were, all I can say is that I was very lucky.

title: 90 percent of the abilities at 25 percent of the price
text:
I have been using these for 6 months and find them to be an excellent value for the money. They perform 90% of the functions of much more expensive earbuds at 25% of the price.

Sound quality is excellent and for most situations the noise canceling is as effective as I require. Unfortunately the only way to reduce this is to only wear one earbud at a time or you will not hear people trying to talk to you.
